name: Cohort AI DevOps Presentation for 3/12
summary:
**Conversation overview**

Michael is co-presenting a cohort webinar with Mumshad Mannambeth (KodeKloud founder) and needed comprehensive research and a polished slide deck showcasing KodeKloud's full AI and AI-enhanced DevOps content ecosystem. The presentation is student-facing, approximately 30-40 minutes, with a hybrid narrative arc — telling the story of how the DevOps engineer role is evolving with AI while embedding a product showcase throughout. Michael works at KodeKloud and has insider knowledge of the platform, which he used to correct Claude's framing at several points.

The conversation moved through three phases: initial research and inventory, iterative slide deck creation and refinement, and a fact-check pass ahead of the presentation. Claude built a 20-slide PPTX deck using PptxGenJS with KodeKloud's dark brand theme (navy background, cyan accents), covering the three-layer framework (Learn AI as a Skill, Learn WITH AI, Practice in Real Environments), AI learning paths, AIOps path, certifications, platform features including the AI Assistant, AI Tutor, and KodeKey, nine AI playgrounds, pricing tiers, platform momentum stats, and a closing next-steps slide. The deck was visually QA'd by converting to PDF and inspecting rendered slide images, catching and fixing a layout overlap on slide 3 before delivery.

Michael provided two significant corrections during the build. First, he pushed back on the AI Learning Path being organized into five tiers (Beginner, Intermediate, Cloud Certs, Advanced, Specialist), calling it unnecessarily complicated and directing Claude to simplify to three generalist tiers — Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced — with certifications slotted by difficulty rather than isolated in their own category (AIF-C01 in Beginner, AI-102 in Advanced, LangChain in Intermediate). Second, he questioned whether the AI Plan was a standalone tier or an add-on, and expressed frustration when Claude asked him rather than looking it up. Claude fetched the live pricing page directly and confirmed the AI Plan is a standalone tier (Standard → Pro → AI → Business), each including everything below it, at $29/mo annual and $99/mo monthly. The slide was updated to show the full tier hierarchy clearly. For the April 9 fact-check pass, the most significant finding was that KodeKey's model lineup had been completely overhauled from 5 to 8 models, adding DeepSeek V3.2, Kimi K2.5 (Moonshot AI), MiniMax M2.1, and Gemini 3 Flash, while upgrading all original providers to newer versions. All pricing, playground counts, learning path structures, and platform features were confirmed unchanged. Michael's clear preference throughout is for Claude to do the research and verify facts directly from live sources rather than asking clarifying questions that he considers the AI's job to answer independently.

input.title: KodeKloud Full AI Ecosystem: A Platform-Wide Inventory of Courses, Tools, and Learning Features
input.content:
# KodeKloud's full AI ecosystem: a platform-wide inventory

**KodeKloud has undergone a brand-level transformation from a "DevOps & Cloud" platform to "DevOps, Cloud & AI," embedding artificial intelligence across every layer — curriculum, tooling, and learning experience.** The platform now offers **~37 distinct AI-related courses**, **2 dedicated AI learning paths**, **9+ AI-specific playgrounds**, and a suite of AI-powered learning features anchored by an AI Assistant, AI Tutor, and unified multi-LLM API key called KodeKey. A dedicated **AI Plan** subscription tier ($29/mo billed annually) bundles all AI capabilities. This inventory covers everything you need to build slides showing the breadth and depth of AI integration across KodeKloud as of March 2026.

---

## CATEGORY 1: Courses and learning paths about AI

### Two structured AI learning paths anchor the curriculum

**The AI Learning Path** (kodekloud.com/learning-path/ai) is KodeKloud's flagship AI offering, containing **19 courses** organized into a progressive tiered structure from beginner to specialist:

- **Beginner tier (Non-Technical Users):** Introduction to OpenAI (~6h 38m) covering transformers, prompt engineering, DALL-E, embeddings, and fine-tuning; Prompt Engineering 101 (~5h), a 100% lab-based course using the open-source Mixtral-8x7B model
- **Intermediate tier (AI for ALL Engineers):** AI-Assisted Development (~6h 20m) covering ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, BlackboxAI, and Tabnine; GitHub Copilot Certification (~5h 34m) with full cert prep; GitHub Copilot in Action (project-based); Cursor AI (~3h 48m); Cline (autonomous VS Code AI toolkit); Claude Code for Beginners; MCP for Beginners (Model Context Protocol); Crash Course: MCP for Beginners
- **Cloud AI Certifications tier:** AWS Certified AI Practitioner (~6h, AIF-C01 exam prep with mock exam); AI-900 Microsoft Azure AI Fundamentals (~4h 10m); AI-102 Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate (~7h 28m)
- **Advanced tier:** Running Local LLMs with Ollama (~2h 15m); Introduction to K8sGPT and AI-Driven Kubernetes Engineering (~1h, covers agentic DevOps future)
- **Specialist tier (Software Developers):** LangChain (building LLM apps with prompts, chains, agents, RAG); PyTorch (deep learning); AI Agents (autonomous agents, multi-agent systems, Claude API, Kubernetes MCP); Mastering Generative AI with OpenAI; NVIDIA Generative AI LLMs Associate Certification (RAG, prompt engineering, model evaluation)

**The AIOps — Learn By Doing path** (kodekloud.com/learning-path/aiops-learn-by-doing) targets DevOps engineers specifically, progressing through: AIOps Foundations with Prometheus & Grafana → Applied ML for AIOps (anomaly detection, log clustering, time-series forecasting) → Automated Remediation with Python → AIOps in Practice (logging and alerting at scale) → Advanced AIOps (distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry & Jaeger) → MLOps for AIOps (MLflow & Kubeflow production workflows).

### The full AI course catalog spans 37+ titles

Beyond the learning paths, additional standalone courses round out the catalog. **AI-Assisted Ansible** pairs Ansible with ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, and Red Hat Ansible Lightspeed for generating, debugging, and optimizing playbooks. **Fundamentals of MLOps** (~5h 8m) covers CI/CD/CT/CM for ML, MLflow, BentoML, and Grafana. **AWS SageMaker** walks through the full ML pipeline from data preparation to hosting. **Fundamentals of RAG** teaches document ingestion, chunking, vector databases, and end-to-end RAG pipelines.

Newer additions (late 2025 through early 2026) include **Building AI Agents with Claude Agent SDK**, **Google ADK (Agent Development Kit)** for multi-agent production apps, **KAgents: Host Your AI Agents on Kubernetes**, **Crash Course: AI-Powered DevOps** (LLMs, MCP, AI agents in CI/CD pipelines), **AI Agents Fundamentals** (introductory), **n8n: Zero to Hero** (workflow automation with AI integration), and **Generative AI in Practice: Advanced Insights & Operations** (Azure AI Studio, adversarial prompting, Prompty tool).

### Five certification prep courses with hands-on labs

KodeKloud offers certification preparation for **AWS Certified AI Practitioner** (AIF-C01), **AI-900 Microsoft Azure AI Fundamentals**, **AI-102 Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate**, **NVIDIA Generative AI LLMs Associate**, and **GitHub Copilot Certification**. The AWS AI Practitioner course also has an associated **cohort program** (kodekloud.com/aws-ai-practitioner-cohort). All include mock exams and hands-on labs. Some courses are also available externally via **Coursera** (AWS AI Practitioner listed at ~14 hours, Introduction to OpenAI).

### Every AI course includes hands-on labs and challenges

This is KodeKloud's core differentiator. The platform hosts dedicated **AI Labs** (kodekloud.com/studio/labs/artificial-intelligence) for building with LLMs and AI frameworks, plus free **AI Crash Course Labs** covering LangChain Basics, LangGraph Basics, RAG Basics, and Gemini CLI. The upcoming **100 Days of AI** challenge program (listed as "Coming Soon" on pricing) will offer structured daily AI tasks similar to their existing "100 Days of DevOps."

---

## CATEGORY 2: AI-enhanced platform features

### The AI Assistant and AI Tutor power personalized learning

**KodeKloud AI Assistant** is embedded directly into hands-on labs across the entire platform. It provides real-time feedback, highlights exactly where things went wrong, assists with troubleshooting Kubernetes pods, Dockerfiles, Terraform plans, and cloud configurations — all without leaving the lab environment. Users interact conversationally ("speak to the AI as you would with a friend"), and it supports **multiple languages**, responding in whatever language the learner uses.

**KodeKloud AI Tutor** is positioned as the "world's first AI-driven personalized hands-on tutor." Announced via LinkedIn in September 2024, it dynamically generates custom learning curricula for any tech topic based on the learner's goals. Key capabilities include **on-demand topic coverage** (generates content for topics not in existing courses), **progress-based adjustments** that adapt to the learner's pace, **seamless topic transitions**, and **real-time task validation**. It's available as a standalone experience at kodekloud.com/courses/ai-tutor.

**AI Assisted Labs** extend the AI Assistant across free labs, crash courses, course labs, and playgrounds — marked as "New!" in current pricing. **Customized Learning Paths** use AI to generate personalized curricula rather than offering one-size-fits-all sequences.

### Nine AI playgrounds provide hands-on sandbox environments

KodeKloud's AI Playgrounds section (kodekloud.com/playgrounds/#AIPlaygrounds) offers sandboxed environments for practicing with real AI tools:

| Playground | What's inside | Session length |
|---|---|---|
| **KodeKey** | Unified API key for GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Grok 3 | Varies by plan |
| **AWS AI** | Bedrock (up to 2 agents), SageMaker, Rekognition, Transcribe, Translate, Comprehend, Textract | Standard |
| **Azure AI** | Azure OpenAI, Azure AI Search, Cognitive Services (Computer Vision, Speech, Text Analytics) | 3 hours |
| **Claude Code** | Anthropic's Claude Code CLI for coding, debugging, refactoring | Standard |
| **OpenAI Codex** | OpenAI's Codex CLI for AI-assisted terminal coding | Standard |
| **Qwen Coder** | Open-source Qwen Coder model for multi-language coding | Standard |
| **MCP** | Model Context Protocol servers with pre-built integrations (Filesystem, PostgreSQL, Google Drive) | 1 hour + extensions |
| **n8n** | Visual low-code automation with KodeKey AI integration for building RAG agents and workflows | Standard |
| **Moonshot** | Listed as AI playground (limited public details) | Standard |

**KodeKey** deserves special attention as a platform-unique innovation. It provides a **single plug-and-play API key** giving instant access to multiple LLMs — eliminating the need to manage separate provider accounts. Free/Standard/Pro plans get **25 credits/month** (500 tokens/credit); the AI Plan gets **500 credits/month** (1,000 tokens/credit). Keys are encrypted and prompts are never stored. KodeKey integrates with other playgrounds (e.g., n8n uses KodeKey for AI API access).

### A dedicated AI subscription tier bundles everything

The **AI Plan** ($99/month, or $29/month billed annually at current 50% promotional pricing) includes everything in the Pro plan plus: Personalized AI Tutor, Multilingual AI, AI Assisted Labs, KodeKey (500 credits/month), Customized Learning Paths, all AI Playgrounds, and the upcoming 100 Days of AI. Even lower-tier plans receive limited KodeKey access (25 credits/month).

### KodeKloud Studio and ai.kodekloud.com

**ai.kodekloud.com** functions as an AI-driven conversational learning interface for DevOps topics including Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD, AWS CLI, and gcloud. **KodeKloud Studio** hosts the AI labs category (kodekloud.com/studio/labs/artificial-intelligence) for hands-on building with LLMs and AI frameworks.

---

## Blog, announcements, and thought leadership

KodeKloud has published **9 dedicated AI blog posts** and **15+ additional posts with significant AI sections**, creating a substantial content library about AI in DevOps. The most strategically important posts include:

**"AI Roadmap 2026 for DevOps & Cloud Engineers"** (December 2025) lays out a 9-step hands-on AI roadmap: AI Fundamentals → MCP & tool integration → AI Agents → LangChain/LangGraph → RAG → Memory/stateful systems → Workflow automation with n8n → AI Playgrounds → MLOps. It argues 2026 belongs to "AI-powered engineers, not tool collectors."

**"MCP Explained Simply: How AI Can Actually Do Things Now"** (July 2025) explains Model Context Protocol as "USB for AI" — the standardized system letting AI models interact with real-world tools. **"How DevOps Can Take Advantage of AI"** covers AI-assisted infrastructure provisioning, AI-powered monitoring, automated testing via NLP, and predictive analytics. The **DevOps Decoded blog series** (Parts 2–4) includes expert Q&A sessions on AI tools in enterprise environments, Kubeflow for ML workloads, and MLOps frameworks.

The **community forum** has a dedicated AI category (kodekloud.com/community/c/ai/59) with a Generative AI subcategory, active since November 2023 with **27+ threads** through March 2026 — covering course support for Claude Code, MCP, AI-900, NVIDIA certification, KodeKey integration questions, and AI agent discussions.

---

## External presence signals full brand commitment to AI

KodeKloud's AI pivot extends across all external channels. The **YouTube channel** (175K+ subscribers) features an **"AI Bites" series** breaking down AI topics for engineers, an MLOps complete guide (~41 min), and Shorts like "3 Must-Have AI Tools for DevOps Teams in 2025" (part of a campaign generating **682K views** and **2.2K new subscribers**). Founder **Mumshad Mannambeth** appeared on **Bret Fisher's podcast** (December 2024, ~74 minutes) discussing how GenAI has changed DevOps certification prep and KodeKloud's AI integration strategy, alongside CTO **Vijin Palazhi**.

On **LinkedIn** (106K+ company followers), KodeKloud formally announced "KodeKloud AI" launch (~September 2024) and the AI Tutor (~September/October 2024). The **mobile app** has been renamed to "KodeKloud: DevOps, Cloud & AI." At **KubeCon North America 2024**, KodeKloud presented on "Future of tech education with AI-Assisted Hands-on Learning." Their **TechWeek Singapore 2026** exhibitor profile explicitly describes KodeKloud as "pioneering the future of tech education with **AI-powered learning paths, intelligent assistants, and hands-on labs**." The platform is now trusted by over **1.5 million learners**.

**G2 reviews** repeatedly cite AI features as a differentiator, with reviewers noting "introduction of AI-powered assistance in labs" and one requesting "agentic AI that acts like a real DevOps assistant." **Nerdisa** and **DevOpsCube** reviews highlight the AI Tutor and AI-assisted labs specifically.

---

## Conclusion: the presentation narrative

The data tells a clear story for your cohort presentation. KodeKloud's AI integration operates on **three simultaneous levels** that mirror how the DevOps engineer role itself is evolving:

1. **Learning AI as a skill** — 37+ courses teach engineers to wield AI tools (Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, MCP, LangChain, AI Agents) and earn AI certifications, reflecting that AI proficiency is now a core DevOps competency
2. **Learning with AI** — the AI Assistant, AI Tutor, and KodeKey transform how engineers learn, with personalized adaptive paths and in-lab mentoring, demonstrating AI as a force multiplier for skill acquisition
3. **Practicing AI in real environments** — 9 AI playgrounds provide sandboxed access to AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Claude Code, MCP servers, and workflow automation tools, letting engineers build muscle memory with the exact tools reshaping production DevOps

The AIOps learning path specifically bridges the gap between traditional DevOps and AI-enhanced operations — from intelligent monitoring with Prometheus/Grafana, to ML-driven anomaly detection, to automated remediation, to MLOps pipelines with MLflow and Kubeflow. Courses like **K8sGPT**, **AI-Powered DevOps**, and **AI Agents on Kubernetes (KAgents)** directly address the agentic DevOps trend. The platform's evolution from a Kubernetes training site to an AI-native learning ecosystem is itself a case study in how AI is reshaping the DevOps landscape.

content.text:
1	---
     2	name: pptx
     3	description: "Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions \"deck,\" \"slides,\" \"presentation,\" or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill."
     4	license: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms
     5	---
     6	
     7	# PPTX Skill
     8	
     9	## Quick Reference
    10	
    11	| Task | Guide |
    12	|------|-------|
    13	| Read/analyze content | `python -m markitdown presentation.pptx` |
    14	| Edit or create from template | Read [editing.md](editing.md) |
    15	| Create from scratch | Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) |
    16	
    17	---
    18	
    19	## Reading Content
    20	
    21	```bash
    22	# Text extraction
    23	python -m markitdown presentation.pptx
    24	
    25	# Visual overview
    26	python scripts/thumbnail.py presentation.pptx
    27	
    28	# Raw XML
    29	python scripts/office/unpack.py presentation.pptx unpacked/
    30	```
    31	
    32	---
    33	
    34	## Editing Workflow
    35	
    36	**Read [editing.md](editing.md) for full details.**
    37	
    38	1. Analyze template with `thumbnail.py`
    39	2. Unpack → manipulate slides → edit content → clean → pack
    40	
    41	---
    42	
    43	## Creating from Scratch
    44	
    45	**Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) for full details.**
    46	
    47	Use when no template or reference presentation is available.
    48	
    49	---
    50	
    51	## Design Ideas
    52	
    53	**Don't create boring slides.** Plain bullets on a white background won't impress anyone. Consider ideas from this list for each slide.
    54	
    55	### Before Starting
    56	
    57	- **Pick a bold, content-informed color palette**: The palette should feel designed for THIS topic. If swapping your colors into a completely different presentation would still "work," you haven't made specific enough choices.
    58	- **Dominance over equality**: One color should dominate (60-70% visual weight), with 1-2 supporting tones and one sharp accent. Never give all colors equal weight.
    59	- **Dark/light contrast**: Dark backgrounds for title + conclusion slides, light for content ("sandwich" structure). Or commit to dark throughout for a premium feel.
    60	- **Commit to a visual motif**: Pick ONE distinctive element and repeat it — rounded image frames, icons in colored circles, thick single-side borders. Carry it across every slide.
    61	
    62	### Color Palettes
    63	
    64	Choose colors that match your topic — don't default to generic blue. Use these palettes as inspiration:
    65	
    66	| Theme | Primary | Secondary | Accent |
    67	|-------|---------|-----------|--------|
    68	| **Midnight Executive** | `1E2761` (navy) | `CADCFC` (ice blue) | `FFFFFF` (white) |
    69	| **Forest & Moss** | `2C5F2D` (forest) | `97BC62` (moss) | `F5F5F5` (cream) |
    70	| **Coral Energy** | `F96167` (coral) | `F9E795` (gold) | `2F3C7E` (navy) |
    71	| **Warm Terracotta** | `B85042` (terracotta) | `E7E8D1` (sand) | `A7BEAE` (sage) |
    72	| **Ocean Gradient** | `065A82` (deep blue) | `1C7293` (teal) | `21295C` (midnight) |
    73	| **Charcoal Minimal** | `36454F` (charcoal) | `F2F2F2` (off-white) | `212121` (black) |
    74	| **Teal Trust** | `028090` (teal) | `00A896` (seafoam) | `02C39A` (mint) |
    75	| **Berry & Cream** | `6D2E46` (berry) | `A26769` (dusty rose) | `ECE2D0` (cream) |
    76	| **Sage Calm** | `84B59F` (sage) | `69A297` (eucalyptus) | `50808E` (slate) |
    77	| **Cherry Bold** | `990011` (cherry) | `FCF6F5` (off-white) | `2F3C7E` (navy) |
    78	
    79	### For Each Slide
    80	
    81	**Every slide needs a visual element** — image, chart, icon, or shape. Text-only slides are forgettable.
    82	
    83	**Layout options:**
    84	- Two-column (text left, illustration on right)
    85	- Icon + text rows (icon in colored circle, bold header, description below)
    86	- 2x2 or 2x3 grid (image on one side, grid of content blocks on other)
    87	- Half-bleed image (full left or right side) with content overlay
    88	
    89	**Data display:**
    90	- Large stat callouts (big numbers 60-72pt with small labels below)
    91	- Comparison columns (before/after, pros/cons, side-by-side options)
    92	- Timeline or process flow (numbered steps, arrows)
    93	
    94	**Visual polish:**
    95	- Icons in small colored circles next to section headers
    96	- Italic accent text for key stats or taglines
    97	
    98	### Typography
    99	
   100	**Choose an interesting font pairing** — don't default to Arial. Pick a header font with personality and pair it with a clean body font.
   101	
   102	| Header Font | Body Font |
   103	|-------------|-----------|
   104	| Georgia | Calibri |
   105	| Arial Black | Arial |
   106	| Calibri | Calibri Light |
   107	| Cambria | Calibri |
   108	| Trebuchet MS | Calibri |
   109	| Impact | Arial |
   110	| Palatino | Garamond |
   111	| Consolas | Calibri |
   112	
   113	| Element | Size |
   114	|---------|------|
   115	| Slide title | 36-44pt bold |
   116	| Section header | 20-24pt bold |
   117	| Body text | 14-16pt |
   118	| Captions | 10-12pt muted |
   119	
   120	### Spacing
   121	
   122	- 0.5" minimum margins
   123	- 0.3-0.5" between content blocks
   124	- Leave breathing room—don't fill every inch
   125	
   126	### Avoid (Common Mistakes)
   127	
   128	- **Don't repeat the same layout** — vary columns, cards, and callouts across slides
   129	- **Don't center body text** — left-align paragraphs and lists; center only titles
   130	- **Don't skimp on size contrast** — titles need 36pt+ to stand out from 14-16pt body
   131	- **Don't default to blue** — pick colors that reflect the specific topic
   132	- **Don't mix spacing randomly** — choose 0.3" or 0.5" gaps and use consistently
   133	- **Don't style one slide and leave the rest plain** — commit fully or keep it simple throughout
   134	- **Don't create text-only slides** — add images, icons, charts, or visual elements; avoid plain title + bullets
   135	- **Don't forget text box padding** — when aligning lines or shapes with text edges, set `margin: 0` on the text box or offset the shape to account for padding
   136	- **Don't use low-contrast elements** — icons AND text need strong contrast against the background; avoid light text on light backgrounds or dark text on dark backgrounds
   137	- **NEVER use accent lines under titles** — these are a hallmark of AI-generated slides; use whitespace or background color instead
   138	
   139	---
   140	
   141	## QA (Required)
   142	
   143	**Assume there are problems. Your job is to find them.**
   144	
   145	Your first render is almost never correct. Approach QA as a bug hunt, not a confirmation step. If you found zero issues on first inspection, you weren't looking hard enough.
   146	
   147	### Content QA
   148	
   149	```bash
   150	python -m markitdown output.pptx
   151	```
   152	
   153	Check for missing content, typos, wrong order.
   154	
   155	**When using templates, check for leftover placeholder text:**
   156	
   157	```bash
   158	python -m markitdown output.pptx | grep -iE "\bx{3,}\b|lorem|ipsum|\bTODO|\[insert|this.*(page|slide).*layout"
   159	```
   160	
   161	If grep returns results, fix them before declaring success.
   162	
   163	### Visual QA
   164	
   165	**⚠️ USE SUBAGENTS** — even for 2-3 slides. You've been staring at the code and will see what you expect, not what's there. Subagents have fresh eyes.
   166	
   167	Convert slides to images (see [Converting to Images](#converting-to-images)), then use this prompt:
   168	
   169	```
   170	Visually inspect these slides. Assume there are issues — find them.
   171	
   172	Look for:
   173	- Overlapping elements (text through shapes, lines through words, stacked elements)
   174	- Text overflow or cut off at edges/box boundaries
   175	- Decorative lines positioned for single-line text but title wrapped to two lines
   176	- Source citations or footers colliding with content above
   177	- Elements too close (< 0.3" gaps) or cards/sections nearly touching
   178	- Uneven gaps (large empty area in one place, cramped in another)
   179	- Insufficient margin from slide edges (< 0.5")
   180	- Columns or similar elements not aligned consistently
   181	- Low-contrast text (e.g., light gray text on cream-colored background)
   182	- Low-contrast icons (e.g., dark icons on dark backgrounds without a contrasting circle)
   183	- Text boxes too narrow causing excessive wrapping
   184	- Leftover placeholder content
   185	
   186	For each slide, list issues or areas of concern, even if minor.
   187	
   188	Read and analyze these images — run `ls -1 "$PWD"/slide-*.jpg` and use the exact absolute paths it prints:
   189	1. <absolute-path>/slide-N.jpg — (Expected: [brief description])
   190	2. <absolute-path>/slide-N.jpg — (Expected: [brief description])
   191	...
   192	
   193	Report ALL issues found, including minor ones.
   194	```
   195	
   196	### Verification Loop
   197	
   198	1. Generate slides → Convert to images → Inspect
   199	2. **List issues found** (if none found, look again more critically)
   200	3. Fix issues
   201	4. **Re-verify affected slides** — one fix often creates another problem
   202	5. Repeat until a full pass reveals no new issues
   203	
   204	**Do not declare success until you've completed at least one fix-and-verify cycle.**
   205	
   206	---
   207	
   208	## Converting to Images
   209	
   210	Convert presentations to individual slide images for visual inspection:
   211	
   212	```bash
   213	python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf output.pptx
   214	rm -f slide-*.jpg
   215	pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 output.pdf slide
   216	ls -1 "$PWD"/slide-*.jpg
   217	```
   218	
   219	**Pass the absolute paths printed above directly to the view tool.** The `rm` clears stale images from prior runs. `pdftoppm` zero-pads based on page count: `slide-1.jpg` for decks under 10 pages, `slide-01.jpg` for 10-99, `slide-001.jpg` for 100+.
   220	
   221	**After fixes, rerun all four commands above** — the PDF must be regenerated from the edited `.pptx` before `pdftoppm` can reflect your changes.
   222	
   223	---
   224	
   225	## Dependencies
   226	
   227	- `pip install "markitdown[pptx]"` - text extraction
   228	- `pip install Pillow` - thumbnail grids
   229	- `npm install -g pptxgenjs` - creating from scratch
   230	- LibreOffice (`soffice`) - PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)
   231	- Poppler (`pdftoppm`) - PDF to images
   232

content.text:
1	# PptxGenJS Tutorial
     2	
     3	## Setup & Basic Structure
     4	
     5	```javascript
     6	const pptxgen = require("pptxgenjs");
     7	
     8	let pres = new pptxgen();
     9	pres.layout = 'LAYOUT_16x9';  // or 'LAYOUT_16x10', 'LAYOUT_4x3', 'LAYOUT_WIDE'
    10	pres.author = 'Your Name';
    11	pres.title = 'Presentation Title';
    12	
    13	let slide = pres.addSlide();
    14	slide.addText("Hello World!", { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, fontSize: 36, color: "363636" });
    15	
    16	pres.writeFile({ fileName: "Presentation.pptx" });
    17	```
    18	
    19	## Layout Dimensions
    20	
    21	Slide dimensions (coordinates in inches):
    22	- `LAYOUT_16x9`: 10" × 5.625" (default)
    23	- `LAYOUT_16x10`: 10" × 6.25"
    24	- `LAYOUT_4x3`: 10" × 7.5"
    25	- `LAYOUT_WIDE`: 13.3" × 7.5"
    26	
    27	---
    28	
    29	## Text & Formatting
    30	
    31	```javascript
    32	// Basic text
    33	slide.addText("Simple Text", {
    34	  x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 2, fontSize: 24, fontFace: "Arial",
    35	  color: "363636", bold: true, align: "center", valign: "middle"
    36	});
    37	
    38	// Character spacing (use charSpacing, not letterSpacing which is silently ignored)
    39	slide.addText("SPACED TEXT", { x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 1, charSpacing: 6 });
    40	
    41	// Rich text arrays
    42	slide.addText([
    43	  { text: "Bold ", options: { bold: true } },
    44	  { text: "Italic ", options: { italic: true } }
    45	], { x: 1, y: 3, w: 8, h: 1 });
    46	
    47	// Multi-line text (requires breakLine: true)
    48	slide.addText([
    49	  { text: "Line 1", options: { breakLine: true } },
    50	  { text: "Line 2", options: { breakLine: true } },
    51	  { text: "Line 3" }  // Last item doesn't need breakLine
    52	], { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 8, h: 2 });
    53	
    54	// Text box margin (internal padding)
    55	slide.addText("Title", {
    56	  x: 0.5, y: 0.3, w: 9, h: 0.6,
    57	  margin: 0  // Use 0 when aligning text with other elements like shapes or icons
    58	});
    59	```
    60	
    61	**Tip:** Text boxes have internal margin by default. Set `margin: 0` when you need text to align precisely with shapes, lines, or icons at the same x-position.
    62	
    63	---
    64	
    65	## Lists & Bullets
    66	
    67	```javascript
    68	// ✅ CORRECT: Multiple bullets
    69	slide.addText([
    70	  { text: "First item", options: { bullet: true, breakLine: true } },
    71	  { text: "Second item", options: { bullet: true, breakLine: true } },
    72	  { text: "Third item", options: { bullet: true } }
    73	], { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 8, h: 3 });
    74	
    75	// ❌ WRONG: Never use unicode bullets
    76	slide.addText("• First item", { ... });  // Creates double bullets
    77	
    78	// Sub-items and numbered lists
    79	{ text: "Sub-item", options: { bullet: true, indentLevel: 1 } }
    80	{ text: "First", options: { bullet: { type: "number" }, breakLine: true } }
    81	```
    82	
    83	---
    84	
    85	## Shapes
    86	
    87	```javascript
    88	slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {
    89	  x: 0.5, y: 0.8, w: 1.5, h: 3.0,
    90	  fill: { color: "FF0000" }, line: { color: "000000", width: 2 }
    91	});
    92	
    93	slide.addShape(pres.shapes.OVAL, { x: 4, y: 1, w: 2, h: 2, fill: { color: "0000FF" } });
    94	
    95	slide.addShape(pres.shapes.LINE, {
    96	  x: 1, y: 3, w: 5, h: 0, line: { color: "FF0000", width: 3, dashType: "dash" }
    97	});
    98	
    99	// With transparency
   100	slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {
   101	  x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,
   102	  fill: { color: "0088CC", transparency: 50 }
   103	});
   104	
   105	// Rounded rectangle (rectRadius only works with ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, not RECTANGLE)
   106	// ⚠️ Don't pair with rectangular accent overlays — they won't cover rounded corners. Use RECTANGLE instead.
   107	slide.addShape(pres.shapes.ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, {
   108	  x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,
   109	  fill: { color: "FFFFFF" }, rectRadius: 0.1
   110	});
   111	
   112	// With shadow
   113	slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, {
   114	  x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 2,
   115	  fill: { color: "FFFFFF" },
   116	  shadow: { type: "outer", color: "000000", blur: 6, offset: 2, angle: 135, opacity: 0.15 }
   117	});
   118	```
   119	
   120	Shadow options:
   121	
   122	| Property | Type | Range | Notes |
   123	|----------|------|-------|-------|
   124	| `type` | string | `"outer"`, `"inner"` | |
   125	| `color` | string | 6-char hex (e.g. `"000000"`) | No `#` prefix, no 8-char hex — see Common Pitfalls |
   126	| `blur` | number | 0-100 pt | |
   127	| `offset` | number | 0-200 pt | **Must be non-negative** — negative values corrupt the file |
   128	| `angle` | number | 0-359 degrees | Direction the shadow falls (135 = bottom-right, 270 = upward) |
   129	| `opacity` | number | 0.0-1.0 | Use this for transparency, never encode in color string |
   130	
   131	To cast a shadow upward (e.g. on a footer bar), use `angle: 270` with a positive offset — do **not** use a negative offset.
   132	
   133	**Note**: Gradient fills are not natively supported. Use a gradient image as a background instead.
   134	
   135	---
   136	
   137	## Images
   138	
   139	### Image Sources
   140	
   141	```javascript
   142	// From file path
   143	slide.addImage({ path: "images/chart.png", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });
   144	
   145	// From URL
   146	slide.addImage({ path: "https://example.com/image.jpg", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });
   147	
   148	// From base64 (faster, no file I/O)
   149	slide.addImage({ data: "image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo...", x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3 });
   150	```
   151	
   152	### Image Options
   153	
   154	```javascript
   155	slide.addImage({
   156	  path: "image.png",
   157	  x: 1, y: 1, w: 5, h: 3,
   158	  rotate: 45,              // 0-359 degrees
   159	  rounding: true,          // Circular crop
   160	  transparency: 50,        // 0-100
   161	  flipH: true,             // Horizontal flip
   162	  flipV: false,            // Vertical flip
   163	  altText: "Description",  // Accessibility
   164	  hyperlink: { url: "https://example.com" }
   165	});
   166	```
   167	
   168	### Image Sizing Modes
   169	
   170	```javascript
   171	// Contain - fit inside, preserve ratio
   172	{ sizing: { type: 'contain', w: 4, h: 3 } }
   173	
   174	// Cover - fill area, preserve ratio (may crop)
   175	{ sizing: { type: 'cover', w: 4, h: 3 } }
   176	
   177	// Crop - cut specific portion
   178	{ sizing: { type: 'crop', x: 0.5, y: 0.5, w: 2, h: 2 } }
   179	```
   180	
   181	### Calculate Dimensions (preserve aspect ratio)
   182	
   183	```javascript
   184	const origWidth = 1978, origHeight = 923, maxHeight = 3.0;
   185	const calcWidth = maxHeight * (origWidth / origHeight);
   186	const centerX = (10 - calcWidth) / 2;
   187	
   188	slide.addImage({ path: "image.png", x: centerX, y: 1.2, w: calcWidth, h: maxHeight });
   189	```
   190	
   191	### Supported Formats
   192	
   193	- **Standard**: PNG, JPG, GIF (animated GIFs work in Microsoft 365)
   194	- **SVG**: Works in modern PowerPoint/Microsoft 365
   195	
   196	---
   197	
   198	## Icons
   199	
   200	Use react-icons to generate SVG icons, then rasterize to PNG for universal compatibility.
   201	
   202	### Setup
   203	
   204	```javascript
   205	const React = require("react");
   206	const ReactDOMServer = require("react-dom/server");
   207	const sharp = require("sharp");
   208	const { FaCheckCircle, FaChartLine } = require("react-icons/fa");
   209	
   210	function renderIconSvg(IconComponent, color = "#000000", size = 256) {
   211	  return ReactDOMServer.renderToStaticMarkup(
   212	    React.createElement(IconComponent, { color, size: String(size) })
   213	  );
   214	}
   215	
   216	async function iconToBase64Png(IconComponent, color, size = 256) {
   217	  const svg = renderIconSvg(IconComponent, color, size);
   218	  const pngBuffer = await sharp(Buffer.from(svg)).png().toBuffer();
   219	  return "image/png;base64," + pngBuffer.toString("base64");
   220	}
   221	```
   222	
   223	### Add Icon to Slide
   224	
   225	```javascript
   226	const iconData = await iconToBase64Png(FaCheckCircle, "#4472C4", 256);
   227	
   228	slide.addImage({
   229	  data: iconData,
   230	  x: 1, y: 1, w: 0.5, h: 0.5  // Size in inches
   231	});
   232	```
   233	
   234	**Note**: Use size 256 or higher for crisp icons. The size parameter controls the rasterization resolution, not the display size on the slide (which is set by `w` and `h` in inches).
   235	
   236	### Icon Libraries
   237	
   238	Install: `npm install -g react-icons react react-dom sharp`
   239	
   240	Popular icon sets in react-icons:
   241	- `react-icons/fa` - Font Awesome
   242	- `react-icons/md` - Material Design
   243	- `react-icons/hi` - Heroicons
   244	- `react-icons/bi` - Bootstrap Icons
   245	
   246	---
   247	
   248	## Slide Backgrounds
   249	
   250	```javascript
   251	// Solid color
   252	slide.background = { color: "F1F1F1" };
   253	
   254	// Color with transparency
   255	slide.background = { color: "FF3399", transparency: 50 };
   256	
   257	// Image from URL
   258	slide.background = { path: "https://example.com/bg.jpg" };
   259	
   260	// Image from base64
   261	slide.background = { data: "image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo..." };
   262	```
   263	
   264	---
   265	
   266	## Tables
   267	
   268	```javascript
   269	slide.addTable([
   270	  ["Header 1", "Header 2"],
   271	  ["Cell 1", "Cell 2"]
   272	], {
   273	  x: 1, y: 1, w: 8, h: 2,
   274	  border: { pt: 1, color: "999999" }, fill: { color: "F1F1F1" }
   275	});
   276	
   277	// Advanced with merged cells
   278	let tableData = [
   279	  [{ text: "Header", options: { fill: { color: "6699CC" }, color: "FFFFFF", bold: true } }, "Cell"],
   280	  [{ text: "Merged", options: { colspan: 2 } }]
   281	];
   282	slide.addTable(tableData, { x: 1, y: 3.5, w: 8, colW: [4, 4] });
   283	```
   284	
   285	---
   286	
   287	## Charts
   288	
   289	```javascript
   290	// Bar chart
   291	slide.addChart(pres.charts.BAR, [{
   292	  name: "Sales", labels: ["Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"], values: [4500, 5500, 6200, 7100]
   293	}], {
   294	  x: 0.5, y: 0.6, w: 6, h: 3, barDir: 'col',
   295	  showTitle: true, title: 'Quarterly Sales'
   296	});
   297	
   298	// Line chart
   299	slide.addChart(pres.charts.LINE, [{
   300	  name: "Temp", labels: ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar"], values: [32, 35, 42]
   301	}], { x: 0.5, y: 4, w: 6, h: 3, lineSize: 3, lineSmooth: true });
   302	
   303	// Pie chart
   304	slide.addChart(pres.charts.PIE, [{
   305	  name: "Share", labels: ["A", "B", "Other"], values: [35, 45, 20]
   306	}], { x: 7, y: 1, w: 5, h: 4, showPercent: true });
   307	```
   308	
   309	### Better-Looking Charts
   310	
   311	Default charts look dated. Apply these options for a modern, clean appearance:
   312	
   313	```javascript
   314	slide.addChart(pres.charts.BAR, chartData, {
   315	  x: 0.5, y: 1, w: 9, h: 4, barDir: "col",
   316	
   317	  // Custom colors (match your presentation palette)
   318	  chartColors: ["0D9488", "14B8A6", "5EEAD4"],
   319	
   320	  // Clean background
   321	  chartArea: { fill: { color: "FFFFFF" }, roundedCorners: true },
   322	
   323	  // Muted axis labels
   324	  catAxisLabelColor: "64748B",
   325	  valAxisLabelColor: "64748B",
   326	
   327	  // Subtle grid (value axis only)
   328	  valGridLine: { color: "E2E8F0", size: 0.5 },
   329	  catGridLine: { style: "none" },
   330	
   331	  // Data labels on bars
   332	  showValue: true,
   333	  dataLabelPosition: "outEnd",
   334	  dataLabelColor: "1E293B",
   335	
   336	  // Hide legend for single series
   337	  showLegend: false,
   338	});
   339	```
   340	
   341	**Key styling options:**
   342	- `chartColors: [...]` - hex colors for series/segments
   343	- `chartArea: { fill, border, roundedCorners }` - chart background
   344	- `catGridLine/valGridLine: { color, style, size }` - grid lines (`style: "none"` to hide)
   345	- `lineSmooth: true` - curved lines (line charts)
   346	- `legendPos: "r"` - legend position: "b", "t", "l", "r", "tr"
   347	
   348	---
   349	
   350	## Slide Masters
   351	
   352	```javascript
   353	pres.defineSlideMaster({
   354	  title: 'TITLE_SLIDE', background: { color: '283A5E' },
   355	  objects: [{
   356	    placeholder: { options: { name: 'title', type: 'title', x: 1, y: 2, w: 8, h: 2 } }
   357	  }]
   358	});
   359	
   360	let titleSlide = pres.addSlide({ masterName: "TITLE_SLIDE" });
   361	titleSlide.addText("My Title", { placeholder: "title" });
   362	```
   363	
   364	---
   365	
   366	## Common Pitfalls
   367	
   368	⚠️ These issues cause file corruption, visual bugs, or broken output. Avoid them.
   369	
   370	1. **NEVER use "#" with hex colors** - causes file corruption
   371	   ```javascript
   372	   color: "FF0000"      // ✅ CORRECT
   373	   color: "#FF0000"     // ❌ WRONG
   374	   ```
   375	
   376	2. **NEVER encode opacity in hex color strings** - 8-char colors (e.g., `"00000020"`) corrupt the file. Use the `opacity` property instead.
   377	   ```javascript
   378	   shadow: { type: "outer", blur: 6, offset: 2, color: "00000020" }          // ❌ CORRUPTS FILE
   379	   shadow: { type: "outer", blur: 6, offset: 2, color: "000000", opacity: 0.12 }  // ✅ CORRECT
   380	   ```
   381	
   382	3. **Use `bullet: true`** - NEVER unicode symbols like "•" (creates double bullets)
   383	
   384	4. **Use `breakLine: true`** between array items or text runs together
   385	
   386	5. **Avoid `lineSpacing` with bullets** - causes excessive gaps; use `paraSpaceAfter` instead
   387	
   388	6. **Each presentation needs fresh instance** - don't reuse `pptxgen()` objects
   389	
   390	7. **NEVER reuse option objects across calls** - PptxGenJS mutates objects in-place (e.g. converting shadow values to EMU). Sharing one object between multiple calls corrupts the second shape.
   391	   ```javascript
   392	   const shadow = { type: "outer", blur: 6, offset: 2, color: "000000", opacity: 0.15 };
   393	   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { shadow, ... });  // ❌ second call gets already-converted values
   394	   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { shadow, ... });
   395	
   396	   const makeShadow = () => ({ type: "outer", blur: 6, offset: 2, color: "000000", opacity: 0.15 });
   397	   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { shadow: makeShadow(), ... });  // ✅ fresh object each time
   398	   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { shadow: makeShadow(), ... });
   399	   ```
   400	
   401	8. **Don't use `ROUNDED_RECTANGLE` with accent borders** - rectangular overlay bars won't cover rounded corners. Use `RECTANGLE` instead.
   402	   ```javascript
   403	   // ❌ WRONG: Accent bar doesn't cover rounded corners
   404	   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.ROUNDED_RECTANGLE, { x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 1.5, fill: { color: "FFFFFF" } });
   405	   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { x: 1, y: 1, w: 0.08, h: 1.5, fill: { color: "0891B2" } });
   406	
   407	   // ✅ CORRECT: Use RECTANGLE for clean alignment
   408	   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { x: 1, y: 1, w: 3, h: 1.5, fill: { color: "FFFFFF" } });
   409	   slide.addShape(pres.shapes.RECTANGLE, { x: 1, y: 1, w: 0.08, h: 1.5, fill: { color: "0891B2" } });
   410	   ```
   411	
   412	---
   413	
   414	## Quick Reference
   415	
   416	- **Shapes**: RECTANGLE, OVAL, LINE, ROUNDED_RECTANGLE
   417	- **Charts**: BAR, LINE, PIE, DOUGHNUT, SCATTER, BUBBLE, RADAR
   418	- **Layouts**: LAYOUT_16x9 (10"×5.625"), LAYOUT_16x10, LAYOUT_4x3, LAYOUT_WIDE
   419	- **Alignment**: "left", "center", "right"
   420	- **Chart data labels**: "outEnd", "inEnd", "center"

content.title: AI Roadmap 2026 for DevOps & Cloud Engineers (Hands-On)
content.text:
An 9-step AI roadmap built for engineers (not data scientists) Step-by-step path from AI fundamentals → MCP → agents → LangChain/LangGraph → RAG → memory → workflow automation (n8n) → AI playgrounds → MLOps.

See how AI is transforming DevOps & Cloud and follow a 2026 roadmap - from fundamentals to agents, RAG, and MLOps with hands-on labs.

To gain a real competitive edge in 2026, you need a roadmap that teaches AI the way real engineers use it - through hands-on, practical workflows. So instead of listing tools, here is a step-by-step AI Roadmap built directly from the KodeKloud AI Labs. This roadmap shows you exactly what to learn, why it matters, and which lab to use at each step. This roadmap takes you from AI fundamentals → automation → agents → real DevOps AI systems. Goal: Understand how AI works so DevOps engineers can apply it. ... Goal: Learn how to provide structured context to AI systems. ... Goal: Use agents to automate tasks like code generation, YAML fixes, log parsing, and CI/CD improvements. ... Goal: Learn how real AI apps are built and orchestrated. ... Goal: Make AI use your documentation, YAMLs, Terraform, logs, configs - not just its training data. ... Goal: Build stateful, context-aware systems.

To stay competitive in 2026, you don’t need to learn every tool. You need a targeted, practical roadmap that bridges DevOps fundamentals with AI-powered workflows. Here’s the simplified, realistic roadmap designed for modern Cloud & DevOps engineers: Before AI can boost your productivity, your fundamentals must be rock-solid. ... Learning becomes easier when you can do it anywhere. The KodeKloud Mobile App lets you practice labs on the go - helping you stay consistent without rearranging your schedule. ... AI can enhance DevOps, but only if you understand the systems it automates. ... If you're planning to get certified, the Linux Foundation Offers give you a rare chance to pursue certifications like CKA, CKAD, CKS, KCSA, LFCS, or KCNA at discounted rates - perfect while strengthening your DevOps fundamentals. ... This is where your competitive edge truly begins.

AI isn’t coming for DevOps jobs - it’s transforming them. The engineers who understand how to work with AI will outperform everyone else in 2026. Here’s what’s changing right now: Instead of waiting for alerts, AI systems analyze logs, metrics, traces, and past incidents to warn you before performance drops or outages occur. ... This reduces manual effort and accelerates releases. AI copilots can help generate Terraform modules, Kubernetes manifests, Helm charts, and Ansible playbooks - reducing human error and boosting speed. AI can automatically detect anomalies, right-size workloads, and suggest cheaper alternatives in real time. AI can scan code, images, pipelines, and cloud configs continuously - catching vulnerabilities long before they reach production. ... The best moment to level up your DevOps & AI skills with Cyber Monday savings.

The best part? No local setup is required. The playground is accessible directly from your browser, so you can start experimenting immediately—no complex configuration or installation needed. An MCP server (Model Context Protocol server) acts as a bridge between AI models and external tools or data sources. It enables AI to access information and perform actions beyond its built-in knowledge. In simple terms, an MCP server translates requests from an AI model into commands that a specific tool or data source can understand and execute. Here are some examples of available MCP servers (the list is always growing): Filesystem: Secure file operations with customizable access controls. PostgreSQL: Read-only database access with the ability to inspect schemas. SQLite: Database interaction and business intelligence features. Google Drive: File access and search capabilities for Google Drive.

Our playground offers an intuitive, hands-on environment to explore the powerful capabilities of Model Context Protocol (MCP) Servers. These servers are designed to simplify and standardize how large language models manage dynamic context.The best part? No local setup is required. The playground is accessible directly from your browser, so you can start experimenting immediately—no complex configuration or installation needed.

Start building smarter, context-aware applications today with the MCP Server Playground!

input.title: KodeKloud AI Ecosystem: Full Fact-Check as of April 9, 2026
input.content:
# KodeKloud AI ecosystem: full fact-check as of April 9, 2026

**The 20-slide deck is largely accurate, but one critical update is needed: KodeKey's model lineup has been completely overhauled.** Five original models (GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Grok 3) have been replaced by eight next-generation models, including GPT-5.2, Claude Sonnet 4.5, and Grok-4. All pricing, playground counts, learning path structures, and platform features remain unchanged. Several new developments since early March — including KubeCon Europe 2026, the "Agentic DevOps" initiative, and a Spring Sale — should be incorporated into the deck.

---

## Pricing holds steady across all four tiers

Every price point on kodekloud.com/pricing matches the deck exactly. No plans have been added, removed, or restructured.

| Plan | Monthly | Annual (per mo) | Annual (billed) | Status |
|------|---------|-----------------|-----------------|--------|
| **Standard** | $35/mo | $15/mo | ~$180/yr | ✅ Confirmed |
| **Pro** | $70/mo | $21/mo | $250/yr | ✅ Confirmed |
| **AI** (marked "Most Popular") | $99/mo | $29/mo | $342/yr | ✅ Confirmed |
| **Business** | Custom quote | Custom quote | Custom quote | ✅ Confirmed |

The **tier hierarchy Standard → Pro → AI → Business** remains intact, with each tier explicitly stating "Everything in [lower tier], plus." The AI plan is currently flagged as "Most Popular" on the pricing page. One note on promotions: KodeKloud is running a **"Skillful Spring Sale" in April 2026**, showing struck-through prices of "45% Off" (Pro) and "50% Off" (AI) on annual billing. However, the displayed annual prices ($21/mo and $29/mo) are the same figures listed across all third-party sources as standard pricing, suggesting these are the regular annual rates and the "discount" framing compares annual-vs-monthly pricing rather than a temporary reduction.

Active coupon codes include **DEVOPSCUBE**, **KKCUBE10** (extra 10% off), and **TRYNOW15** (one-time 15% off). A **KubeCon Europe 2026 merch promotion** runs through June 28, 2026, for booth visitors who purchase an Annual Pro or AI plan. The platform also offers a **30-day money-back guarantee** on all plans.

**"100 Days of AI" remains "Coming Soon."** It is listed under AI plan features as "100 Days of AI (Coming Soon) *Unlimited tasks." No dedicated landing page exists for it yet. The companion programs — 100 Days of DevOps and 100 Days of Cloud — are both fully live and active.

---

## AI Learning Path still at 19 courses, with new additions outside the path

The **AI Learning Path** at kodekloud.com/learning-path/ai holds at exactly **19 courses**, organized across five sections:

- **Beginner (2):** Introduction to OpenAI, Prompt Engineering 101
- **Intermediate — AI for ALL Engineers (6):** AI-Assisted Development, GitHub Copilot Certification, Cursor AI, Claude Code for Beginners (8h 32m), MCP for Beginners (2h 31m), Cline
- **Cloud AI Fundamentals (4):** AWS Certified AI Practitioner, AI-900 Azure AI Fundamentals, AI-102 Azure AI Engineer Associate, AWS SageMaker
- **Specialist: DevOps/Platform Engineers (3):** Fundamentals of MLOps, Running Local LLMs With Ollama, K8sGPT & AI-Driven Kubernetes Engineering
- **Specialist: Software Developers (4):** LangChain, PyTorch, AI Agents (5h 18m, by Gav Ridgeway), NVIDIA Generative AI LLMs Associate Certification

The **AIOps Learn By Doing path** retains its **6-course structure**: AIOps Foundations (Prometheus & Grafana), AIOps in Practice (Logging & Alerting), Automated Remediation with Python, Applied ML for AIOps (Anomaly Detection & Forecasting), Advanced AIOps (Distributed Tracing & Root Cause Analysis), and MLOps for AIOps (MLflow & Kubeflow).

All nine specifically named courses from the deck are verified as existing on the platform:

| Course | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| AI Agents | ✅ In AI Learning Path | 5h 18m, by Gav Ridgeway |
| Building AI Agents with Claude Agent SDK | ✅ Standalone | May still have "notify when released" form |
| Google ADK | ✅ Standalone | Appears on recent courses page |
| KAgents: Host Your AI Agents on Kubernetes | ✅ Listed | Explicitly labeled **"Coming Soon"** |
| Crash Course: AI-Powered DevOps | ✅ Standalone | By Mumshad Mannambeth; covers Agentic DevOps |
| MCP for Beginners | ✅ In AI Learning Path | 2h 31m |
| n8n: Zero to Hero | ✅ Standalone | By Marconi Darmawan; GitHub repo updated Mar 3, 2026 |
| AI-Assisted Ansible | ✅ Standalone | By Andrei Balint; covers ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude Code, Ansible Lightspeed |
| Claude Code for Beginners | ✅ In AI Learning Path | 8h 32m |

Additional AI courses found outside the main learning path include **Fundamentals of RAG**, **AI Agents Fundamentals** (separate from "AI Agents"), **Mastering Generative AI with OpenAI**, and the **AI Tutor** course page. The **total AI course count of 37+** could not be independently verified to an exact figure. Counting 19 (AI path) + 6 (AIOps path) + approximately 8–12 standalone AI courses yields a range of **33–37**, making the "37+" claim plausible but at the upper edge. No new AI certification prep courses beyond the existing five (AWS AI Practitioner, AI-900, AI-102, NVIDIA GenAI LLMs, GitHub Copilot) were found.

---

## KodeKey models upgraded from 5 to 8 — the deck's biggest required update

This is the **single most significant change** requiring a deck update. The KodeKey page at kodekloud.com/ai-playgrounds/kodekey now lists **8 models**, replacing all 5 original models with newer versions and adding 3 entirely new providers:

| Model | Provider | Replaces | Token Limit (Std/Pro) | Token Limit (AI Plan) |
|-------|----------|----------|----------------------|----------------------|
| **GPT-5.2** | OpenAI | GPT-4o & GPT-4.1 | 457K | 914K |
| **Gemini 3 Pro** | Google | Gemini 2.5 Pro | 526K | 1.1M |
| **Claude Sonnet 4.5** | Anthropic | Claude Sonnet 4 | 417K | 833K |
| **Grok-4** | xAI | Grok 3 | 417K | 833K |
| **Gemini 3 Flash** | Google | *New addition* | 2.1M | 4.2M |
| **DeepSeek V3.2** | DeepSeek | *New addition* | 13.5M | 27M |
| **Kimi K2.5** | Moonshot AI | *New addition* | 2.1M | 4.2M |
| **MiniMax M2.1** | MiniMax | *New addition* | 5.2M | 10.3M |

The four original providers (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, xAI) all received model version upgrades. Three entirely new providers were added: **DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax**. The GitHub repo at kodekloudhub/kodekey-examples has **not yet been updated** and still references the old model identifiers (claude-sonnet-4, gpt-4o, etc.), confirming the update is recent.

The **KodeKey credit structure** remains: **25 credits/month** on Standard/Pro plans (lower token limits), **500 credits/month** on the AI plan (higher token limits). Credits renew on the 1st of every month. All plans get API key access, all models, and real-time usage tracking; the AI plan adds priority support.

---

## All 9 AI playgrounds confirmed, platform features unchanged

Every playground from the deck is verified as present on kodekloud.com/playgrounds:

1. **KodeKey** ✅ — Now with 8 models (see above)
2. **AWS AI** ✅
3. **Azure AI** ✅ (URL path: azure-openai)
4. **Claude Code** ✅
5. **OpenAI Codex** ✅
6. **Qwen Coder** ✅
7. **MCP** ✅
8. **n8n** ✅
9. **Moonshot** ✅

**No new AI playgrounds have been added** beyond these nine. The platform lists **78 total playgrounds** across all categories (including non-AI playgrounds for Linux, Kubernetes, Docker, etc.).

All AI platform features are confirmed active and unchanged:

- **AI Assistant** remains embedded in labs, providing real-time feedback, troubleshooting help, and multilingual support
- **AI Tutor** is still available as "the world's first AI-driven personalized hands-on tutor" with customized learning paths, on-demand topic coverage, and progress-based adjustments
- **AI Assisted Labs** are still listed (marked "New!" on the pricing page) and exclusive to AI plan and above
- **Customized Learning Paths** remain an AI plan exclusive feature
- **Multilingual AI** is confirmed as an AI plan feature

---

## March–April 2026 brought KubeCon, Agentic DevOps, and a Spring Sale

**KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe 2026** (March 23–26, Amsterdam) was KodeKloud's largest recent public presence, with **booth #792** and three delivered sessions: Abhinav Sharma on backend-first IDPs with Argo CD and Crossplane; Mumshad Mannambeth and Michael Forrester on Kubernetes releases and SIGs; and Nourhan Mohamed on AI on Kubernetes with reproducible ML environments. Abhinav Sharma also spoke at the co-located **Platform Engineering Day EU 2026** on Protocol-First architecture in the age of Agentic AI.

The most notable conceptual development is KodeKloud's push into **"Agentic DevOps."** Mumshad Mannambeth announced on LinkedIn in late March/early April that KodeKloud is hosting a live session in April explaining what Agentic DevOps means for working engineers, with practical frameworks and a getting-started playbook. His messaging frames existing DevOps skills (Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD, Terraform) as "the foundation that AI agents need to operate" — agents run on Kubernetes and deploy through CI/CD pipelines. This represents a new strategic positioning that bridges KodeKloud's DevOps core with its AI expansion.

**Three cohort programs** are actively running with bi-weekly check-ins (Check-in #3 was March 12, 2026): the **Kubestronaut Cohort** (5 CNCF Kubernetes certs), the **Golden Kubestronaut Cohort** (15 total CNCF certs), and the **AWS AI Practitioner Cohort**. All use private Discord channels, leaderboards at cohorts.kodekloud.com, and live check-in sessions.

**Platform statistics** as of April 2026: **1.5M+ learners** (confirmed on the KubeCon 2026 page as "Trusted by over 1.5M engineers"), **180+ premium courses** (up from ~174 during the February Free Learning Week), **1,280+ hands-on labs**, **78 playgrounds**, **5,000+ hours of content**, and a **50K+ Discord community**. The G2 rating stands at **4.8/5 stars** across **960 reviews**, with multiple Leader badges in Virtual IT Labs, Online Course Providers, Corporate LMS, and Technical Skills Development categories across enterprise, EMEA, Asia, and India segments.

---

## Conclusion: what to update in the deck

The deck requires **one critical update** and several minor additions. The KodeKey model slide must be completely revised — five models replaced by eight, with three new providers (DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, MiniMax) and version upgrades across all four original providers. All pricing figures, plan structures, playground counts, learning path structures, and AI feature descriptions remain accurate and need no changes.

For new additions worth incorporating: the KubeCon Europe 2026 presence with three sessions demonstrates growing industry credibility; the "Agentic DevOps" initiative signals KodeKloud's strategic direction for bridging DevOps and AI agent orchestration; the learner count has reached **1.5M+** (verify whether the deck uses this or an older figure); and the total course catalog has grown to **180+** with **1,280+ labs**. The **KAgents** course (coming soon) and **Building AI Agents with Claude Agent SDK** are the newest course additions to watch. The "100 Days of AI" remains unreleased.
